  with global-error.tsx (both previously exported GlobalError)

Reduces image attack surface by not installing unnecessary recommended
packages alongside gcc and libpq-dev in the backend Docker image.

- backend/Dockerfile: combined non-root user security fix with start.sh entrypoint,
  merged consecutive RUN instructions into one layer
- frontend/components/modules/cae-tab.tsx: took main's rewrite (sub-tabs + useToast),
  our alert() fix was already absorbed
- PROGRESS.md: manual merge, appended Etape 14

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ci: add type-check script and suppress S1128 for app pages

- Add `type-check` npm script (tsc --noEmit) to frontend/package.json
  to catch type-import regressions like the Phase 8 RequiredFieldsForm/CalendarView incident
- Suppress typescript:S1128 (unused imports) on frontend/app/** in sonar-project.properties
  to avoid false positives on JSX component imports that SonarCloud misidentifies as type-only

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: restore runtime imports stripped by Phase 8 type-import cleanup

RequiredFieldsForm and CalendarView are JSX components, not TypeScript types.
Marking them as `type` imports causes the bundler to strip them at compile
time, resulting in a ReferenceError at render. Removed the `type` keyword.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: replace remaining alert() calls in dashboard/boards/[id]/page.tsx

7 native alert() calls missed in the previous commit, now replaced with
useToast notifications following the same pattern as the rest of the app.

files with proper UI: toast notifications (useToast) for alerts, and Shadcn
Dialog with controlled state for confirmation dialogs.

repository.name: racecontrol

commit.message:
perf: Phase 1 — 5 critical optimizations

1. findTraderAcrossSources request-scoped cache — eliminates N+1 queries
   on trader detail pages (8 redundant DB calls → 1). Saves ~400-800ms.

2. getTraderStats Redis cache + eliminate redundant captured_at sub-query.
   Was the only trader query function without caching. Saves ~300-600ms.

3. AnimatedROI count-up animation limited to top 3 hero rows only.
   Prevents 100 concurrent rAF loops (3000 state updates). Fixes mobile jank.

4. Pagination disabled button contrast fix — remove opacity:0.4 (WCAG 1.4.3
   violation, ~2.7:1 ratio), use tokens.colors.text.disabled instead.
   Also strengthen :focus-visible with !important to prevent inline override.

5. Top 3 trader avatar preload on homepage via <link rel="preload">.
   Saves ~200ms on LCP by starting avatar download before render.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: v2026.5.18 — Mesh networking fully operational with peer approval system

Router integration: chat() and chatStream() now check mesh peers when local provider fails.
Peer approval: discovered peers land in pending queue, user approves via dashboard or CLI.
Supports up to 5 connected peers with maxPeers config. Auto-approve toggle available.
Dynamic heartbeat updates model lists every 60s. allowRemoteModels/maxRemoteTasks enforced.
New CLI: --pending, --approve, --reject, --revoke, --auto-approve.
New API: /api/mesh/pending, /api/mesh/approve/:id, /api/mesh/reject/:id, /api/mesh/revoke/:id.
Dashboard broadcasts mesh_peer_discovered/approved/connected/revoked events.
Approved peers persisted to ~/.titan/approved-peers.json across restarts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Initial release — complete RAG evaluation suite (v0.1.0)

- Core package with 7 modules: config, dataset, retrieval, generation, chunking, runner, report
- 4 retrieval metrics: recall@k, precision@k, MRR, NDCG@k (pure Python, zero dependencies)
- 4 generation metrics: faithfulness, relevance, completeness, answer_length_ratio (heuristic-based)
- 3 chunking strategies: fixed-size, sentence-based, paragraph-based with comparison tool
- Built-in TF-IDF keyword retriever for zero-dependency baseline evaluation
- Click CLI: ragtest run, ragtest chunk, ragtest metrics
- Rich terminal output with color-coded scores + optional HTML reports
- YAML-first config and dataset format
- 54 tests across 5 test files, all passing
- pip-installable via pyproject.toml with click, pyyaml, rich dependencies
- MIT License

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: ragtest
repository.description: RAG evaluation suite — benchmark retrieval accuracy, generation quality, and chunking strategies. pytest-style test runner for RAG pipelines.

commit.message:
Fix migration: remove non-IMMUTABLE cast from index expression

PostgreSQL requires index expressions to be IMMUTABLE, but
text::timestamptz depends on timezone settings (STABLE). Index on the
raw text value instead — ISO 8601 strings sort correctly as text.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Use journalctl subprocess for journal following

The systemd crate's direct journal API couldn't read system journal
files because the process lacks the systemd-journal group membership
that the setgid journalctl binary has. Spawning journalctl as a
subprocess fixes this and also drops the libsystemd C dependency.

commit.message:
feat!: C26 — comprehensive multi-expert audit & improvement (5 iterations)

5 expert agents analyzed the codebase (API accuracy, architecture,
code quality, security, CCXT compatibility), followed by 5 iteration
cycles of targeted improvements:

Iter1 - CRITICAL fixes:
- GRVT EIP712 chainId: Ethereum 1/5 → ZKsync 325/326822723
- Extended StarkNet chainId: string → hex format
- Aster fetchMarkets weight: 40 → 1
- All 16 adapters: add explicit `implements IExchangeAdapter`
- All 16 normalizers: add normalizeSymbol() + toExchangeSymbol()

Iter2 - Normalizer completeness:
- GMX: normalizeBalance, normalizeOrderBook (NotSupportedError)
- Jupiter: normalizeOrder, normalizeTrade
- Ostium: normalizeOrderBook + normalizeFundingRate (NotSupportedError)
- GRVT: normalizeFundingRate
- Nado: normalizeMarket

Iter3 - Coverage + type safety:
- DriftClientWrapper: 56% → 100% (+30 tests)
- GmxAuth: 76% → 100% (+12 tests)
- DriftNormalizer: 62% → 70% branches (+15 tests)
- High-risk `as any` documented with eslint-disable reasons

Iter4 - Security hardening:
- Ostium: removed unused private key storage (BREAKING)
- dYdX: documented placeholder address derivation limitation
- Jupiter: Pyth Hermes RPC fallback URL array
- Lighter WASM signer: security notes added

Iter5 - CCXT parity:
- supportsFeature() promoted to public API + IExchangeAdapter interface
- Hyperliquid: fetchOpenOrders missing from FeatureMap → fixed
- 13 adapters: explicit false entries for unsupported methods

BREAKING CHANGES:
- OstiumAuth no longer accepts/stores privateKey
- supportsFeature() now part of IExchangeAdapter interface

Tests: 6297 passed (+56), 0 failed, 0 coverage threshold violations
TypeScript: 0 errors | ESLint: 0 errors | Build: PASS

commit.message:
Security hardening: fix audit findings from Milestone 7.1

- Fix TOCTOU race on MAX_CONCURRENT: wrap count+insert in sql.begin()
  transaction with FOR UPDATE locks to prevent concurrent bypass
- Fix material duplication: consume materials atomically inside the
  creation transaction (DELETE from inventory), not just check them
- Fix prompt injection: sanitize player name/class before LLM system
  prompt — strip non-alphanumeric, cap at 30 chars

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Switch nav from position:fixed to position:sticky to fix iOS Safari 26 gap

The root cause: Safari 26 Liquid Glass recalculates the viewport when the
bottom toolbar collapses, causing fixed elements to shift down and leave a
transparent gap at the top. Sticky positioning lives in the document flow
and is immune to this viewport desync.

Also changed overflow-x from hidden to clip on html/body to avoid breaking
sticky behavior, and removed the nav__bg workaround layer.

* Add UDP-to-Kafka pipeline: .NET 10 worker service with PacketMapper, KafkaProducer, and UdpListenerService

- Scaffolds TelemetryIngester worker service (net10.0, C# 14) with Serilog, Confluent.Kafka, F1Game.UDP
- Configuration classes for all four appsettings sections (Telemetry, Kafka, TimescaleDb, Ingester)
- Canonical event records: CarTelemetryEvent, LapDataEvent, CarStatusEvent (nullable merge approach)
- PacketMapper maps F1Game.UDP structs to canonical events; player-car-only and all-cars modes
- KafkaProducer routes events to car-telemetry/lap-data/car-status topics; fire-and-forget semantics
- KafkaMessageSerializer wraps events in the canonical JSON envelope {eventType, ..., data: {...}}
- UdpListenerService BackgroundService: receive → decode → map → produce; session-change detection
- 15 unit tests covering field mapping, tyre temp ordering, DRS conversion, player/all-cars filtering, sector time combination, and JSON envelope structure

commit.message:
v2.6.3 - Fix upgrade check failing with misleading "no internet" error

get_latest_release() piped the GitHub API response through jq. If jq
is not installed the pipe fails silently (2>/dev/null), $latest is
empty, and the function returns 1 — indistinguishable from a real
network failure, printing "Could not reach GitHub."

Fix: use grep+sed as the primary JSON parser (zero extra deps) with
jq as an optional enhancement when available. Also improved the two
distinct error messages so network failures vs parse failures are
distinguishable.

commit.message:
feat: ATK route optimizer — Z-jog elimination with DRC safety checks

Post-routing optimizer that removes unnecessary Z-shaped jogs
(H→V(short)→H or V→H(short)→V) from maze router output, replacing
them with cleaner L-shapes.

Safety checks prevent DRC violations:
- Other-net M2.b/M1.b spacing against signal segments
- Infrastructure obstacle avoidance (power M2 vbars, access M2 pads)
- Same-net gap detection (close-but-not-touching shapes)
- Anchor point protection (vias at junction points)

Result: 6 Z-jogs eliminated (250→244 segments), DRC 41 (unchanged),
LVS PASS. Integrated into run_all.sh as Phase 4b.

feat: OSS simulation workflow improvements (#10)

## Summary

End-to-end improvements for open-source FPGA simulation workflows
(Verilator, Icarus, yosys+nextpnr):

- **Fix Verilator & Icarus backends** — log files now written, pass/fail
detection scans testbench output (not just exit codes), `--binary
--timing` for Verilator 5+, extra_args forwarded as env vars
- **Cocotb support for Verilator** — uses cocotb's own `verilator.cpp`
main with VPI event loop (`--cc --exe --prefix Vtop`), wires up all
required env vars (`GPI_USERS`, `COCOTB_TEST_MODULES`, `PYTHONPATH`,
etc.)
- **Simulator auto-detection** — `--tool` defaults to `auto`, probes
xsim → verilator → icarus so OSS-only users don't need `--tool
verilator` every time
- **Hardened pass/fail detection** — `SimOutputScan::is_pass()` requires
exit 0 + no FAIL patterns + non-empty output + evidence of completion
(`PASS:`/`$finish`/`PASSED`), eliminating silent failures
- **`--waves` CLI flag** — opt-in waveform tracing (Verilator `--trace`,
Icarus `-lxt2`)
- **Environment check improvements** — Verilator 5.0+ version floor
enforced, `vvp` runtime verified for Icarus, cocotb version helpers
added
- **yosys/nextpnr output parsing** — structured extraction of resource
utilization, timing, and Fmax from tool output with progress events and
stderr capture

### Files changed (19 files, +1451/-67)

| Area | Files |
|------|-------|
| Verilator backend | `loom-verilator/src/lib.rs`, `env_check.rs` |
| Icarus backend | `loom-icarus/src/lib.rs`, `env_check.rs` |
| Shared sim infra | `loom-core/src/util.rs`, `sim/compat.rs`,
`sim/runner.rs`, `plugin/simulator.rs` |
| CLI | `loom-cli/src/commands/sim.rs`, `watch.rs`, `build.rs`, `ui.rs`
|
| yosys/nextpnr | `loom-yosys/src/synth.rs`, `pnr.rs`, `pack.rs`,
`lib.rs`, `output_parser.rs` |
| Examples | `sim_demo/lib/alu/tb/test_alu.py`,
`fifo/tb/tb_fifo_basic.sv` |

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------
